comment: Write-routing contract for any agent working in engineering-os (Claude Code, local LLMs, MCP clients). Reading is unified: every vault in vaults.json is indexed together for retrieval. Writing is segregated by content type. vault_id values match vaults.json. Copy to rag/routing.json to activate.

routes.description: The agent's own identity, its rules for working with this user, and its observations about the collaboration.
rules: identity/ files define who the agent is in this working relationship. Edit them only when the change IS an identity change, not as a session log.
Routine notes about how a session went, patterns noticed, or corrections received go to observations/.

routes.description: Durable facts about the user: communication style, local environment, stances. Not events, not tasks, not the mood of the day.
rules: Agents NEVER write directly into communication/, environment/, or facts/. Those namespaces are curated.
Stage new material in _inbox/ with frontmatter: type: candidate-fact, source: <where it surfaced>, evidence: <verbatim quote or reference>.
The underscore prefix keeps _inbox/ out of retrieval until a human promotes the note into a curated namespace.

routes.description: Work knowledge: runbooks, cost reviews, pipelines, controls, ADRs, weekly reviews.
rules: Route to the most specific domain. New architecture decisions go to Architecture/decisions/ as sequentially numbered ADRs.
A durable fact about the user that surfaces in an operational note is ALSO staged to the user vault _inbox/ as a separate candidate; the operational note stays in the work vault.

decision_order: 1. Is it the agent's own identity or a note about the collaboration? Route to the ai vault.
2. Is it a durable fact about the user, independent of today's events? Stage in the user vault _inbox/.
3. Is it dated, actionable, or bound to a work domain? Route to the work vault.
4. Unsure? Default to the work vault and tag the note needs-routing.
